verbatim: Stage 1 is stateless (JSON-only output: blacklist + per-layer budgets). Re-running is cheap and always safe.
invariant_implied: Stage 1 has no resume state; rerun is idempotent
claim_summary: Stage 1 stateless / idempotent — JSON-only outputs

verbatim: Per-layer atomic checkpointing to `_stage2_partial/`: merge_{layer_idx}.json: centroid IDs, groupings, frequencies, merge map; layer_{layer_idx}.pt: covariance snapshot for this layer
invariant_implied: Layer completion = both .json and .pt persisted atomically
claim_summary: Stage 2 per-layer atomic checkpoints (.json + .pt)

verbatim: On resume, completed layers are replayed from partial files (fast, no forward pass). The model must be passed in pre-merge state (Stage 1 output) — a guard checks num_routed_experts matches the pre-merge count.
invariant_implied: Resume requires pre-merge model; num_routed_experts guard validates input state
claim_summary: Stage 2 resume guards model state via num_routed_experts check

verbatim: Critical invariant: Covariance remapping (_remap_covariance_for_layer) must happen BEFORE the snapshot. Snapshotting before remapping persists pre-merge expert keys, corrupting Stage 3 inputs on resume.
invariant_implied: Remap must precede snapshot to avoid pre-merge key persistence
claim_summary: Stage 2 covariance remap-before-snapshot invariant

verbatim: After each layer's accumulation is finalised, both B and C covariances are spilled to disk (`_stage3_bcov_partial/` and `_stage3_ccov_partial/`). Background I/O thread overlaps spill with the next batch's forward pass, keeping the resident footprint bounded.
invariant_implied: Per-layer spill of B/C covariances bounds memory and supplies resume points
claim_summary: Stage 3 per-layer covariance spill (background I/O)

verbatim: B-cov spill files at _stage3_bcov_partial/layer_{idx}.pt — layers whose spill files already exist are skipped on re-entry
invariant_implied: Existence of layer_{idx}.pt B-cov implies layer collection complete
claim_summary: Stage 3 B-cov spills skipped on re-entry

verbatim: Spill directory is cleaned up on successful Stage 3 completion
invariant_implied: Successful completion deletes spill directory; presence of spill = incomplete or in-progress
claim_summary: Stage 3 spill dir cleaned on success

verbatim: Original weights snapshot (_stage3_original_weights.pt) is saved for Stage 4 residual computation
invariant_implied: Originals snapshot durably available for downstream Stage 4
claim_summary: Stage 3 originals snapshot for Stage 4 ΔW

verbatim: Per-layer atomic checkpointing to _stage4_partial/layer_{layer_idx}.pt (format_version=1). Each checkpoint contains the full FactoredExperts U/V state, ranks, effective ranks, and parameter counts. On resume, completed layers are loaded directly; failed layers re-run from the Stage 3 output.
invariant_implied: Stage 4 per-layer atomic .pt with format_version=1; failed layers re-run from Stage 3 output
claim_summary: Stage 4 atomic per-layer .pt with format_version=1

verbatim: Stage 4 deletes _stage3_original_weights.pt and _stage2_input_covariance.pt on success (both are already durable on per-stage Hub repos).
invariant_implied: Sidecar deletion safe because Hub commits already preserve durability
claim_summary: Stage 4 sidecar deletion relies on Hub durability

verbatim: Step-boundary checkpointing to _stage5_partial/step_{N}.pt (every 100 optimizer steps). Each checkpoint contains router parameter state + optimizer state. On resume, the last incomplete batch's gradient signal is silently dropped (no accumulation window — each batch is one optimizer step). Only the two most recent checkpoints are retained.
invariant_implied: Step-boundary checkpoints every 100 steps; rolling window of 2; partial batch grads discarded on resume
claim_summary: Stage 5 step-boundary checkpoint every 100 steps; rolling window=2

verbatim: Stage 6 is stateless. Re-running is always safe. The teacher eval cache persists across runs (it's a JSON file in the artifacts directory, also uploaded to Hub).
invariant_implied: Stage 6 stateless; teacher_eval_cache.json is speedup not resume; uploaded to Hub
claim_summary: Stage 6 stateless; teacher cache durable on Hub

verbatim: HF Jobs bucket FUSE mounts are not durable under SIGKILL or timeout. The durability boundary is per-stage Hub uploads
invariant_implied: Bucket FUSE = non-durable; only per-stage Hub upload is durable
claim_summary: Per-stage Hub upload is the durability boundary; bucket non-durable

verbatim: Each heavy stage (2–5) uploads its checkpoint to a per-stage Hub repo immediately on completion. The bucket is treated as scratch cache only.
invariant_implied: Each heavy stage commits to its own Hub repo upon completion; bucket = scratch only
claim_summary: Stages 2-5 commit to per-stage Hub repos; bucket = scratch cache

verbatim: All partial checkpoint files are written via .tmp → os.replace (atomic on POSIX). A SIGKILL mid-write leaves at most a .tmp file, never a truncated final file. Dangling .tmp files are cleaned up at stage startup.
invariant_implied: Write-to-tmp + os.replace; dangling .tmp files are cleaned at startup; final files never truncated
claim_summary: Atomic .tmp+os.replace idiom; dangling .tmp cleaned at startup

verbatim: --no-resume flag: When passed to run_pipeline.py, disables all within-stage resume behaviour. Each stage runs unconditionally from scratch with no partial-file I/O. Stage 1 and Stage 6 are unaffected (they have no resume files).
invariant_implied: --no-resume disables partial I/O for stages 2-5; stages 1 and 6 unaffected
claim_summary: --no-resume bypasses stages 2-5 partial I/O

verbatim: 3 | _stage3_bcov_partial/, _stage3_ccov_partial/ spills; _stage3_alpha_result.json | Per covariance phase + α search | Delete existing spills; skip α cache
invariant_implied: Stage 3 resume keyed on spill files + α-result JSON; --no-resume deletes spills and skips α cache
claim_summary: Stage 3 resume granularity: covariance phase + α search

verbatim: Covariance remapping (_remap_covariance_for_layer) happens BEFORE the snapshot (_snapshot_cov_layer), which happens BEFORE the merge JSON write (_write_merge_json). A layer is considered complete only when BOTH .json and .pt exist. If .pt exists without .json (orphaned by crash between snapshot and JSON write), the .pt is deleted and the layer is reprocessed from scratch.
invariant_implied: Layer-completion = both .json AND .pt present; orphan .pt without .json => delete and reprocess
claim_summary: Stage 2 layer-completion invariant: .pt+.json both required; orphan .pt deleted

verbatim: On re-entry, if all per-layer B-cov spill files exist in _stage3_bcov_partial/, Phase A (covariance collection) is skipped entirely — including the teacher model load (~70 GB, ~60s). The α search result is cached in _stage3_alpha_result.json and reused on re-entry (~33 min saved).
invariant_implied: Complete spill set => skip Phase A and teacher load; α-result JSON cached for reuse
claim_summary: Stage 3 re-entry skips Phase A on full spill set; α cached

verbatim: _stage3_original_weights.pt is saved immediately after _snapshot_originals() returns, BEFORE the α search and Phase C factoring. Stage 4 can access originals even if Stage 3 crashes during factoring.
invariant_implied: Originals snapshot is the durability boundary for Stage 4 ΔW; persists across Stage 3 crashes
claim_summary: Stage 3 originals snapshot saved before α/factoring; survives Stage 3 crash

verbatim: Stage 4 double-widen guard: When _stage3_original_weights.pt is absent but stage4_eora/eora_ranks.json exists, Stage 4 detects a double-widen attempt and raises AssertionError. This protects against in-process re-runs (notebooks, test harnesses) where widen_rank() would silently double-apply EoRA correction.
invariant_implied: Originals absent + eora_ranks present => already widened; refuse to widen again
claim_summary: Stage 4 double-widen guard via originals/eora_ranks invariant

verbatim: Stage 5 deferred teacher load: The teacher model is loaded lazily on the first live batch (after fast-forward completes), not before the training loop. This eliminates wasted load time on resume.
invariant_implied: Teacher loaded only after fast-forward to last completed step; lazy to save resume time
claim_summary: Stage 5 lazy teacher load eliminates resume waste

verbatim: Every partial checkpoint carries a format_version field. On resume, the version is checked before any state is restored. A mismatch raises an error with an actionable message ("delete _stage{N}_partial/ and re-run"). This prevents silent corruption when checkpoint format changes across code versions.
invariant_implied: format_version field required on every partial; mismatch => hard error with actionable message
claim_summary: format_version enforced on every partial checkpoint

verbatim: Results and param counts are saved to teacher_eval_cache.json with a cache key = sha256(model_name + revision + eval_config_subset).
invariant_implied: Teacher eval cache key = sha256(model_name+revision+eval_config); auto-invalidates on change
claim_summary: Teacher eval cache uses sha256 cache key for auto-invalidation

verbatim: Auto-invalidation: If the model name, revision, or any eval config parameter changes, the cache key mismatches and the teacher is re-evaluated.
invariant_implied: Any change in model name/revision/eval config invalidates cache automatically
claim_summary: Teacher cache auto-invalidates on any model/eval-config change
